checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary validation at STATE-LENGTH-VALID supporting ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH

reachability.notes: Attacker can invoke precompile via on-chain transaction with arbitrary calldata length.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Trace data flow from boundary ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH through node STATE-LENGTH-VALID.
2. Property context: The secp256r1 precompile input must be exactly 160 bytes. Inputs of any other length must cause the precompile to return empty output without reverting.
3. Verify that STATE-LENGTH-VALID correctly validates/processes data that has crossed the trust boundary.
4. Check internal invariants are maintained even if boundary validation is partially bypassed.
5. Test with edge-case inputs that pass boundary validation but stress internal handling.
executable_checks: Trace data flow from ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH to STATE-LENGTH-VALID
Verify STATE-LENGTH-VALID handles edge cases from boundary input
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W0-EIP7951-PRECOND-001. Supporting node for boundary ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-CHECK-FORMAT: A DataColumnSidecar received from the P2P network must have its cell count match

reachability.notes: Attacker sends sidecar with wrong cell count, potentially causing buffer overflows or incorrect KZG verification.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Identify the trust boundary at EDGE-CHECK-FORMAT (trust level: UNTRUSTED, target: unknown).
2. Property: A DataColumnSidecar received from the P2P network must have its cell count match the expected number of cells per column before acceptance.
3. Target interface: N/A.
4. Attack vectors: generic boundary fuzzing.
5. Craft adversarial inputs crossing this boundary to test validation completeness.
6. Verify all input validation, authentication, and data sanitization are enforced.
7. Check for bypass paths that skip validation.
8. Severity justification: Mismatched cell count could cause memory corruption or incorrect proof verification in all nodes, potentially crashing >33% of the network.
executable_checks: Fuzz EDGE-CHECK-FORMAT with malformed inputs crossing the trust boundary
Verify input validation completeness for unknown
Test boundary with maximum-size and minimum-size payloads
Check for consensus-splitting behavior across different client implementations
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W1-DASCORE-PRECOND-002. Boundary edge: EDGE-CHECK-FORMAT. Trust level: UNTRUSTED. Target: unknown.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-RECEIVE-STATUS: Before accepting a peer status message during eth/69 handshake, the receiving no

reachability.notes: Untrusted P2P peers can initiate handshake with arbitrary networkid

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Identify the trust boundary at EDGE-RECEIVE-STATUS (trust level: UNTRUSTED, target: unknown).
2. Property: Before accepting a peer status message during eth/69 handshake, the receiving node MUST validate that the peer's networkid matches the local node's networkid. Mismatched network IDs MUST result in immediate connection termination to prevent cross-network attacks.
3. Target interface: N/A.
4. Attack vectors: generic boundary fuzzing.
5. Craft adversarial inputs crossing this boundary to test validation completeness.
6. Verify all input validation, authentication, and data sanitization are enforced.
7. Check for bypass paths that skip validation.
8. Severity justification: Cross-network confusion could allow attackers to connect mainnet nodes with testnet nodes, potentially causing consensus failures or eclipse attacks affecting >33% of network if widely exploited.
executable_checks: Fuzz EDGE-RECEIVE-STATUS with malformed inputs crossing the trust boundary
Verify input validation completeness for unknown
Test boundary with maximum-size and minimum-size payloads
Check for consensus-splitting behavior across different client implementations
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W2-EIP7642-HANDSHAKE-PRECOND-01. Boundary edge: EDGE-RECEIVE-STATUS. Trust level: UNTRUSTED. Target: unknown.

checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary validation at STATE-DATA-COLUMN-SIDECAR-REJECTED supporting EDGE-GOSSIP-RECEIVE

reachability.notes: First validation barrier against malformed gossip data

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Trace data flow from boundary EDGE-GOSSIP-RECEIVE through node STATE-DATA-COLUMN-SIDECAR-REJECTED.
2. Property context: Before accepting a data_column_sidecar from gossip (EDGE-GOSSIP-RECEIVE boundary), the node MUST run verify_data_column_sidecar to check: sidecar.index < NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS, len(kzg_commitments) > 0, l
3. Verify that STATE-DATA-COLUMN-SIDECAR-REJECTED correctly validates/processes data that has crossed the trust boundary.
4. Check internal invariants are maintained even if boundary validation is partially bypassed.
5. Test with edge-case inputs that pass boundary validation but stress internal handling.

Verify STATE-DATA-COLUMN-SIDECAR-REJECTED handles edge cases from boundary input

checklist_item.title: Falsify Post-condition on ACTION-GET-PROPOSER-INDICES: After proposer lookahead initialization, each entry in the proposer_lo

reachability.notes: Invalid proposer index would cause block proposals to fail or be rejected.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Post-condition: After proposer lookahead initialization, each entry in the proposer_lookahead vector must reference a valid, active validator index for the corresponding slot's epoch.
2. Execute the operation with various valid inputs.
3. After each execution, verify:
   a. All expected side-effects occurred correctly
   b. No unexpected state changes occurred
   c. Return values match the post-condition specification
4. Test with boundary inputs that are valid but extreme.
5. Check partial execution/failure scenarios.
6. Severity justification: Invalid proposer indices would cause missed slots and attestation failures for affected nodes.
executable_checks: Execute operation on ACTION-GET-PROPOSER-INDICES and verify all post-condition guarantees
Check for unexpected side-effects after operation completion
Test partial failure scenarios for post-condition violations
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W3-FULU-LOOKAHEAD-POST-001. Type: Post-condition. Primary element: ACTION-GET-PROPOSER-INDICES.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-PARENT-TO-CALC: The parent block header must contain valid excess_blob_gas and blob_gas_used fie

reachability.notes: A malicious peer can propagate blocks with crafted blob gas fields. If validation is insufficient during BPO fork transition, incorrect excess blob gas could be computed.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Identify the trust boundary at EDGE-PARENT-TO-CALC (trust level: UNTRUSTED, target: unknown).
2. Property: The parent block header must contain valid excess_blob_gas and blob_gas_used fields before excess blob gas calculation. Both fields must be non-negative and blob_gas_used must not exceed max blob gas (GAS_PER_BLOB * blob_schedule.max).
3. Target interface: N/A.
4. Attack vectors: generic boundary fuzzing.
5. Craft adversarial inputs crossing this boundary to test validation completeness.
6. Verify all input validation, authentication, and data sanitization are enforced.
7. Check for bypass paths that skip validation.
8. Severity justification: Invalid parent blob gas fields accepted during BPO fork transition could cause consensus splits affecting >33% of nodes if different clients compute different excess blob gas values.
executable_checks: Fuzz EDGE-PARENT-TO-CALC with malformed inputs crossing the trust boundary
Verify input validation completeness for unknown
Test boundary with maximum-size and minimum-size payloads
Check for consensus-splitting behavior across different client implementations
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W4-EIP7892-PRECOND-002. Boundary edge: EDGE-PARENT-TO-CALC. Trust level: UNTRUSTED. Target: unknown.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Pre-condition on ACTION-COMPUTE-WORDS: The multiplication complexity computation must use ceil(max_length / 8

reachability.notes: Floor vs ceil division produces different word counts for non-multiple-of-8 lengths, leading to different gas costs across clients.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Pre-condition: The multiplication complexity computation must use ceil(max_length / 8) for word calculation, not floor or truncating division. Rounding differences would produce different gas costs.
2. Enumerate inputs that should be rejected by this pre-condition.
3. Attempt bypass with:
   a. Invalid/malformed inputs that may slip through validation
   b. Edge case values (zero, max, negative, empty)
   c. Type confusion or encoding tricks
4. Verify correct rejection of all invalid inputs.
5. Check if bypassing leads to exploitable state.
6. Severity justification: Rounding difference in word computation causes gas cost divergence between clients, leading to consensus split.
executable_checks: Attempt precondition bypass on ACTION-COMPUTE-WORDS with crafted invalid inputs
Test edge-case values (zero, max, overflow) against precondition
Verify rejection behavior is consistent across all code paths
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W4-EIP7883-PRECOND-003. Type: Pre-condition. Primary element: ACTION-COMPUTE-WORDS.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Post-condition on ACTION-UPGRADE-TO-FULU: After upgrade_to_fulu completes, the state fork object MUST have curre

reachability.notes: Incorrect fork version in state would cause all post-fork signature verification to fail, splitting the network.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Post-condition: After upgrade_to_fulu completes, the state fork object MUST have current_version == FULU_FORK_VERSION (0x06000000), previous_version == ELECTRA_FORK_VERSION, and epoch == FULU_FORK_EPOCH.
2. Execute the operation with various valid inputs.
3. After each execution, verify:
   a. All expected side-effects occurred correctly
   b. No unexpected state changes occurred
   c. Return values match the post-condition specification
4. Test with boundary inputs that are valid but extreme.
5. Check partial execution/failure scenarios.
6. Severity justification: Wrong fork version causes all post-fork block/attestation signatures to be computed against incorrect domain, crashing consensus for all nodes.
executable_checks: Execute operation on ACTION-UPGRADE-TO-FULU and verify all post-condition guarantees
Check for unexpected side-effects after operation completion
Test partial failure scenarios for post-condition violations
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W6-FULU-FORK-ST-POSTCOND-001. Type: Post-condition. Primary element: ACTION-UPGRADE-TO-FULU.

reachability.notes: Untrusted P2P peer can send mismatched array lengths. Missing check could cause out-of-bounds access.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:

2. Property context: All input arrays to verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch (commitments_bytes, cell_indices, cells, proofs_bytes) MUST have equal length.
3. Verify that ACTION-VALIDATE-BATCH-INPUTS correctly validates/processes data that has crossed the trust boundary.
4. Check internal invariants are maintained even if boundary validation is partially bypassed.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-PEER-SEND-BLOCK: A block received from a P2P peer must have its RLP-encoded size checked against

reachability.notes: Attacker sends oversized block via P2P to exhaust node resources if size check is not performed early.

Verify that invalid inputs are rejected with appropriate error handling (no crashes, no partial processing). 5. Test boundary values and edge cases specific to this check.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for KZG Cell Proof Verification in DataColumnSidecar

reachability.notes: Attacker sends sidecar with invalid KZG proofs to inject corrupted data or exploit proof verification bugs.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Craft a DataColumnSidecar with valid format and valid inclusion proof but invalid KZG cell proofs. 2. Send via P2P gossip to target CL nodes. 3. Verify the sidecar is rejected at the KZG verification step. 4. Test with: (a) random bytes as proofs, (b) valid BLS points that don't match commitments, (c) proofs from a different column, (d) proofs for wrong cells. 5. Verify peer scoring penalty is applied. 6. Verify the sidecar is not stored or forwarded.
executable_checks: Craft sidecar with swapped KZG proofs between columns
Send invalid sidecar and verify rejection + peer penalty
Verify no partial storage of invalid sidecar data
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-DASCORE-PRECOND-004. Boundary edge EDGE-RECEIVE-SIDECAR / EDGE-CHECK-KZG. Trust level: UNTRUSTED. This is the core DAS security guarantee - forging KZG proofs breaks data availability.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Block Serving Within Advertised Range After BlockRangeUpdate

reachability.notes: Node must serve blocks within the range it advertised via BlockRangeUpdate.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Observe a node's BlockRangeUpdate message to determine its advertised range [earliestBlock, latestBlock]. 2. Request blocks within this range and verify they are served correctly. 3. Request blocks outside this range and verify appropriate error response. 4. Test at range boundaries: earliestBlock-1 and latestBlock+1. 5. Verify range updates correctly after new blocks are received.
executable_checks: Query blocks at advertised range boundaries
Verify correct error for out-of-range requests
Monitor BlockRangeUpdate consistency
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-EIP7642-HISTORY-POSTCOND-01. Internal property (Post-condition), Formal Verification mindset. EIP-7642 BlockRangeUpdate protocol correctness.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Fork Digest XOR Computation Correctness

reachability.notes: Fork digest = ForkData(fork_version, genesis_validators_root) truncated to 4 bytes.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Compute the Fulu fork digest using FULU_FORK_VERSION and the genesis_validators_root for mainnet. 2. Compare across all CL client implementations. 3. Verify the digest is computed as compute_fork_digest(FULU_FORK_VERSION, genesis_validators_root). 4. Verify the SSZ hash_tree_root of ForkData is computed correctly. 5. Test with different genesis_validators_root values to ensure no hardcoding.
executable_checks: Compute Fulu fork digest across all CL clients
Compare results byte-for-byte
Verify no hardcoded digest values
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-FULU-FORK-DIGEST-POST-001. Internal property (Post-condition), Formal Verification mindset. Incorrect fork digest causes total network partition.

checklist_item.title: Falsify ModExp Zero-Padding Behavior for Short Calldata

reachability.notes: Short calldata with large declared lengths: different zero-padding implementations could diverge.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with declared base_length=32, exponent_length=32, modulus_length=32 but provide only 96+10 bytes of calldata (missing 86 bytes). 2. Verify all clients zero-pad the missing bytes identically. 3. Test with declared lengths >> actual calldata (e.g., declared 1024 bytes each, only 96 bytes provided). 4. Test with zero-length calldata. 5. Cross-client comparison of the ModExp result.
executable_checks: Call ModExp with short calldata and large declared lengths
Cross-client comparison of results
Test with calldata shorter than 96 bytes (missing length declarations)
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W4-EIP7883-POSTCOND-005. Internal property (Post-condition), Formal Verification mindset. Related ambiguity AMB-EIP7883-02. Zero-padding divergence is a known consensus split vector.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for DataColumnSidecar Peer Request Validation

reachability.notes: Malicious peer can send crafted sidecar requests to cause errors or resource exhaustion.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Send DataColumnSidecarsByRange request with slot range outside retention window. 2. Verify ResourceUnavailable error. 3. Send request with column index >= NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS. 4. Verify error without crash. 5. Send rapid requests exceeding rate limit. 6. Verify rate limiting enforcement. 7. Send request with start_slot=0, count=MAX_UINT64.
executable_checks: Send requests with out-of-range slots and column indices
Verify error responses without node crash
Test rate limiting with rapid requests
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6-FULU-VAL-RETAIN-PRECOND-002. Boundary edge EDGE-P2P-SIDECAR-REQUEST. Trust level: UNTRUSTED. Malformed P2P requests must not crash the node.

checklist_item.title: Verify unexpected state changes after fork choice block application
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Capture store's justified and finalized checkpoints before applying block via fork choice. 2. Create test blocks with various checkpoint states: newer justified only, newer finalized only, both newer, both older, equal checkpoints. 3. Apply block and snapshot resulting store checkpoints. 4. Verify post-condition: if block state has newer checkpoints, store must update; if not newer, store must not change. 5. Test for unexpected side-effects: unintended checkpoint rollbacks, partial updates (justified but not finalized), updates when checkpoints are equal. 6. Document any scenario where store checkpoints diverge from expected post-condition.
executable_checks: Apply block with newer justified checkpoint - verify store.justified updates
Apply block with newer finalized checkpoint - verify store.finalized updates
Apply block with both newer checkpoints - verify both update
Apply block with older checkpoints - verify store does not change
Apply block with equal checkpoints - verify store does not change
Apply block with newer justified but older finalized - verify only justified updates
Test checkpoint rollback scenario - verify store never regresses to older values
Verify no other store state is modified beyond justified/finalized checkpoints
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W0-FULUFC-POSTCOND-001 covering fork choice checkpoint update logic

checklist_item.title: Verify continuation to next validation stage after block size check passes
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Craft blocks with size exactly at MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE and below. 2. Submit for validation and verify size check passes (EDGE-SIZE-OK). 3. Verify STATE-BLOCK-SIZE-VALID is reached. 4. Monitor state machine to ensure next validation stage is invoked. 5. Check no premature termination or unexpected state. 6. Verify all validation stages execute in correct sequence. 7. Test edge cases at boundary (MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE - 1, MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE). 8. Confirm no side-effects like early acceptance without full validation.
executable_checks: Submit block with size = MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE and verify further validation invoked
Submit block with size = MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE - 1 and verify progression
Verify EDGE-SIZE-OK transition leads to STATE-BLOCK-SIZE-VALID
Check that next validation stage (signature/header/transaction) is executed
Monitor validation pipeline for premature termination after size check
Verify no early block acceptance without completing remaining checks
Test that STATE-BLOCK-SIZE-VALID does not become a terminal state
Confirm validation log shows progression through all expected stages
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W1-EIP7934-POSTCOND-002 covering nodes=[STATE-BLOCK-SIZE-VALID], edges=[EDGE-SIZE-OK]

checklist_item.title: Falsify Post-condition: Response sidecars not in strict ascending order
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Populate local DA storage with multiple sidecars across different slots and column indices. 2. Send DataColumnSidecarsByRange request covering multiple slots and columns. 3. Capture the response stream. 4. Parse all returned sidecars and extract (slot, column_index) tuples. 5. Verify strict ascending order: for each consecutive pair (s1, c1) and (s2, c2), verify s1 < s2 OR (s1 == s2 AND c1 < c2). 6. Test edge cases: same slot with multiple columns, single slot, single column, maximum range.
executable_checks: Request DataColumnSidecarsByRange spanning multiple slots and verify response is ordered by slot ascending
Request DataColumnSidecarsByRange for single slot with multiple columns and verify column_index ascending order
Parse response stream and verify no (slot, column_index) pair violates strict ascending order
Request maximum range and verify ordering is maintained across entire response
Verify no duplicate (slot, column_index) pairs in response
Test response with single sidecar and verify trivial ordering
Request range with sparse sidecars (gaps in slot/column) and verify returned subset is ordered
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W2-FULU-REQRESP-POSTCOND-01 covering node STATE-RANGE-RESPONSE-SENT and edge EDGE-RANGE-SEND

checklist_item.title: Falsify Proposer Lookahead Safety Invariant Through Targeted Attack Simulation
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Compute proposer lookahead for current epoch E, obtain list of proposers for epochs E through E+MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD+1. 2. Identify validators scheduled as proposers in far-future slots. 3. Simulate attack scenarios enabled by early proposer knowledge: targeted DoS, validator exit manipulation, MEV supply chain attacks, stake manipulation. 4. Measure attack success rate with lookahead vs. baseline. 5. Quantify if extended advance notice materially increases attack surface. 6. Check if any new attack vector is feasible that was not possible with standard 1-epoch notice.
executable_checks: Simulate targeted DoS: identify proposer for slot E+MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD+1, measure time available for attack preparation
Adversary with 10% stake uses lookahead to selectively exit validators not chosen as future proposers, measure impact
MEV actor uses lookahead to pre-negotiate with all known proposers, measure centralization risk increase
Compare attack feasibility with 1-epoch lookahead (baseline) vs MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD+1 lookahead
Test if lookahead enables long-range coordination attacks between colluding validators
Verify no proposer-targeting attack becomes economically viable solely due to extended lookahead window
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W3-FULU-BEACON-LOOKAHEAD-INV-003 covering STATE-PROPOSER-KNOWN

checklist_item.title: Verify BlockValidation excess_blob_gas pre-validation defense-in-depth
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Review BlockValidation code for excess_blob_gas checks. 2. If redundant validation exists, confirm it matches P2P layer. 3. If no redundant validation, document reliance on P2P pre-validation. 4. Test internal API bypass: call BlockValidation directly with invalid excess_blob_gas.
executable_checks: Code review: identify all excess_blob_gas validation points in BlockValidation
Direct API call: invoke BlockValidation with invalid excess_blob_gas, bypassing P2P layer
Defense-in-depth test: verify BlockValidation either re-validates or documents trust assumption
Specification review: confirm EIP defines validation responsibility at P2P boundary
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W4-EIP7892-PRECOND-006 covering BlockValidation node

checklist_item.title: Falsify Lookahead Enforcement: Trigger On-The-Fly Proposer Computation After Initialization
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Initialize proposer_lookahead array at epoch boundary. 2. Monitor all code paths that retrieve proposer for a slot. 3. Instrument get_beacon_proposer to detect computation vs array lookup. 4. Trigger edge cases: cache invalidation, state corruption, out-of-bounds slot access. 5. Verify if any code path computes proposer instead of reading from lookahead.
executable_checks: After lookahead initialization, corrupt the lookahead array and verify get_beacon_proposer fails rather than computing
Request proposer for slot outside current epoch range to test fallback behavior
Trigger deep reorg crossing epoch boundary and verify lookahead is reinitialized not recomputed per-slot
Simulate validator set changes mid-epoch and verify lookahead remains authoritative
Inject state transition forcing cache eviction and verify lookahead is read from persistent storage not recomputed
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W6-FULU-FORK-CON-INV-001 covering get_beacon_proposer

checklist_item.title: Falsify BLS G1 Subgroup Check: Inject Invalid Curve Points via Commitment Deserialization
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Craft commitment bytes representing points not on BLS G1 curve or not in subgroup. 2. Submit via P2P (blob sidecars, data columns). 3. Trace bytes_to_kzg_commitment deserialization. 4. Verify if subgroup check is performed. 5. Test bypass: points on curve but wrong subgroup, invalid field elements, points at infinity. 6. Confirm if invalid points reach cryptographic operations causing consensus divergence.
executable_checks: Submit commitment bytes encoding a point on curve but not in BLS G1 subgroup and verify rejection
Provide commitment with invalid field element (>= BLS modulus) and verify deserialization failure
Send point at infinity encoded in commitment bytes and verify subgroup check handling
Test commitment bytes in uncompressed format when compressed expected and verify validation
Inject commitment bytes passing structural checks but failing subgroup test and verify rejection before verification
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W7-PCS-VERIFY-PRECOND-004 covering bytes_to_kzg_commitment

checklist_item.title: Falsify proposer_lookahead vector size invariant across epoch transitions

reachability.notes: Incorrect vector size could cause index-out-of-bounds or wrong proposer assignment.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Process epoch transitions and verify proposer_lookahead has exactly 2 * SLOTS_PER_EPOCH entries after each epoch processing. 2. Attempt to violate: process multiple consecutive epochs and check for vector growth or shrinkage. 3. Test fork initialization: verify the vector contains entries for remaining current epoch + lookahead epochs. 4. Test edge case: epoch transition at fork boundary where lookahead format changes.
executable_checks: Epoch test: after process_epoch, assert len(state.proposer_lookahead) == 2 * SLOTS_PER_EPOCH
Fork init test: after upgrade_to_fulu, assert proposer_lookahead covers correct slot range
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-EIP7917-INV-001. Formal Verification mindset: attempting to find epoch transitions that violate the size invariant.

checklist_item.title: Falsify MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE constant consistency across clients

reachability.notes: Different clients using different MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE values would cause consensus divergence.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Verify MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE == 8,388,608 (8 MiB = 8 * 1024 * 1024) in each client implementation. 2. Construct a block with RLP size exactly 8,388,608 bytes and verify acceptance. 3. Construct a block with RLP size 8,388,609 bytes and verify rejection. 4. Cross-client: submit boundary-size block and verify identical accept/reject decision.
executable_checks: Cross-client: verify constant value 8388608 in source code
Test: block at exactly 8388608 bytes RLP, assert accepted
Test: block at 8388609 bytes RLP, assert rejected
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-EIP7934-INV-001. Invariant: constant must be exactly 8 MiB.

checklist_item.title: Falsify fork transition epoch boundary requirement

reachability.notes: Fork at a non-epoch boundary would corrupt state transition logic.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Attempt to trigger fork upgrade at a slot that is NOT an epoch boundary (slot % SLOTS_PER_EPOCH != 0). 2. Verify the upgrade is rejected. 3. Trigger upgrade at exact epoch boundary and verify success. 4. Test: slot = SLOTS_PER_EPOCH * N - 1 (one before boundary), slot = SLOTS_PER_EPOCH * N (at boundary), slot = SLOTS_PER_EPOCH * N + 1 (one after).
executable_checks: Test: upgrade_to_fulu at non-epoch-boundary slot, assert rejection
Test: upgrade_to_fulu at epoch boundary, assert success
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-FULU-FORK-TRANSITION-PRE-001. Pre-condition: slot must be at epoch boundary.

checklist_item.title: Falsify ModExp gas cost floor of 500 across all input combinations

reachability.notes: Attacker could craft inputs targeting the minimum gas path. If the floor is bypassed, extremely cheap ModExp calls enable DoS.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Call ModExp with minimal inputs: base_length=0, exponent_length=0, modulus_length=0. Verify gas cost >= 500. 2. Call with base_length=1, exponent_length=1, modulus_length=1 with all-zero values. Verify gas cost >= 500. 3. Fuzz: generate random (base_length, exponent_length, modulus_length) tuples, verify gas cost is always >= 500. 4. Cross-client: verify all implementations enforce the 500 floor identically.
executable_checks: Test: ModExp(0,0,0) gas cost >= 500
Test: ModExp(1,1,1) with zero values, gas cost >= 500
Fuzz: 10000 random inputs, assert gas >= 500 for all
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W4-EIP7883-INV-001. Invariant: gas floor of 500 must hold for ALL inputs.

checklist_item.title: Falsify proposer_lookahead initialization ordering at fork boundary

reachability.notes: Race condition where block validation occurs before lookahead is initialized could cause undefined behavior.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. At the exact Fulu fork transition, verify proposer_lookahead is fully initialized BEFORE any block proposal or validation occurs. 2. Attempt to falsify: submit a block for validation at the first post-fork slot and verify the lookahead is populated. 3. Test: check if there's a window between fork upgrade and lookahead initialization where block validation could be attempted.
executable_checks: Test: at first post-fork slot, verify state.proposer_lookahead is non-empty
Test: block validation at first post-fork slot succeeds (lookahead available)
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6-FULU-FORK-CON-PRECOND-001. Related ambiguity: AMB-FULU-FORK-03. Pre-condition: lookahead must be initialized before use.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Pectra fork activation epoch comparison

reachability.notes: Off-by-one in epoch comparison could cause premature or delayed fork activation.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Verify Pectra activation does NOT occur at epoch 364031 (PECTRA_ACTIVATION_EPOCH - 1). 2. Verify Pectra activation DOES occur at epoch 364032 (PECTRA_ACTIVATION_EPOCH). 3. Verify activation persists at epoch 364033. 4. Cross-client: verify all clients use >= comparison with the same constant 364032 for mainnet.
executable_checks: Test: epoch 364031, assert Electra rules
Test: epoch 364032, assert Pectra rules
Cross-client: verify PECTRA_ACTIVATION_EPOCH == 364032 in all clients
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-EIP7600-FORK-PRECOND-001. Pre-condition: current_epoch >= 364032 for mainnet Pectra activation.

checklist_item.title: Falsify equivalence of reconstructed and gossip-received column handling
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Obtain a set of data columns via gossip for a known block. 2. Independently reconstruct the same columns from sufficient erasure-coded pieces. 3. Compare the internal representation: (a) data content must be byte-identical, (b) metadata (block_root, column_index, slot) must match, (c) KZG proofs must be identical. 4. Verify both gossip-received and reconstructed columns pass the same validation pipeline. 5. Check if any internal flags or status fields differentiate reconstructed from received columns (they should not for downstream processing).
executable_checks: Reconstruct columns and compare byte-for-byte with gossip-received equivalents
Verify reconstructed columns pass the same is_data_available check
Check internal data structures for any differentiation between sources
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-DASCORE-INV-005. Invariant falsification. Related ambiguity: AMB-DAS-CORE-04. Inconsistent treatment could cause DA sampling failures.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for DataColumnSidecarsByRange request parameter validation
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Send a DataColumnSidecarsByRange request with: (a) count = MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS + 1 (exceeds limit), (b) start_slot = 0, count = 2^64-1 (overflow), (c) columns list containing duplicate indices, (d) columns list containing out-of-range indices (>= NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS), (e) empty columns list. 2. Verify each request is rejected with appropriate error and does not cause resource exhaustion. 3. Measure response time for valid large requests near the limit. 4. Verify the node rate-limits repeated large requests from the same peer.
executable_checks: Send request with count exceeding MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS, verify rejection
Send request with overflow values, verify no crash
Send request with out-of-range column indices, verify rejection
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-FULU-REQRESP-PRECOND-01. Boundary property. Trust boundary: untrusted peer sends request parameters. Unbounded requests could exhaust disk I/O and memory.

checklist_item.title: Falsify proposer lookahead ordering after all other epoch processing steps
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Inspect the process_epoch function in each CL client. 2. Verify process_proposer_lookahead (or equivalent) is called AFTER: (a) process_justification_and_finalization, (b) process_rewards_and_penalties, (c) process_slashings, (d) process_sync_committee_updates. 3. Construct a test case where rewards/slashings change the active validator set. 4. Compute the lookahead with correct ordering vs incorrect ordering (before rewards). 5. Verify the results differ, confirming ordering matters. 6. Check all CL clients for consistent ordering.
executable_checks: Audit process_epoch call order in each CL client
Construct state where rewards change active set, verify lookahead depends on order
Compare lookahead results with correct vs incorrect ordering
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-FULU-EPOCH-PROC-INV-002. Invariant falsification. Wrong ordering produces different proposer assignments on all buggy nodes.

checklist_item.title: Verify reconstruction output is re-verified against blob KZG commitments
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. After reconstruction completes, verify the reconstructed blob data is validated against the original blob_kzg_commitments from the beacon block. 2. If any reconstructed blob fails KZG commitment verification, verify the entire reconstruction is rejected. 3. This is a defense-in-depth check: even if individual column KZG passes, the full blob commitment must also be verified.
executable_checks: Verify post-reconstruction KZG commitment check exists
Supply valid-KZG columns that reconstruct to wrong blob, verify rejection
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7594-RECON-PRECOND-001. Supporting node check. Defense-in-depth: reconstruction output must be verified against blob commitments.

checklist_item.title: Falsify EIP-6110 implementation completeness before history expiry activation
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Verify the CL client processes deposits via EIP-6110 (in-protocol deposits from execution layer) rather than reading deposit contract logs from historical EL data. 2. After history expiry: attempt to process a new validator deposit. Verify it succeeds via EIP-6110. 3. Verify the CL does NOT attempt to read historical deposit contract logs (which would fail after history expiry). 4. Check for any code paths that still reference the deposit contract log method. 5. Test: process a deposit after the EL has pruned pre-merge history.
executable_checks: Process validator deposit after history expiry, verify success via EIP-6110
Verify no code path reads deposit contract logs post-history-expiry
Code audit: search for legacy deposit log reading code
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6-EIP7927-ACT-INV-002. Invariant falsification. Related assumption: ASSUM-EIP7927-03. Missing EIP-6110 implementation combined with history expiry would halt all new validator activations.

checklist_item.title: Verify finalized slot computation uses correct checkpoint epoch for boundary validation
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Verify compute_start_slot_at_epoch(store.finalized_checkpoint.epoch) is used (not store.finalized_checkpoint.epoch directly). 2. Test at epoch boundaries: if finalized at epoch 10 (slot 320), verify slot 320 is rejected and slot 321 is accepted. 3. Test with non-zero SLOTS_PER_EPOCH offsets to ensure start-of-epoch computation is correct.
executable_checks: Assert finalized slot is computed as compute_start_slot_at_epoch(finalized_checkpoint.epoch)
Assert comparison is strictly greater-than (not >=)
Assert boundary is correct at epoch transitions
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-FULUFC-PRECOND-003. Supporting node check for ACTION-CHECK-AFTER-FINALIZED. Off-by-one in slot comparison or incorrect epoch-to-slot conversion could allow finalized-slot blocks.

checklist_item.title: Falsify RLP block size limit enforcement during block construction
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Construct a block that is close to MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE (fill with large transactions). 2. Verify the block builder checks RLP-encoded size before finalizing. 3. Attempt to add one more transaction that would push the block over MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE. 4. Verify the builder stops adding transactions. 5. Verify the final RLP-encoded block size is <= MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE. 6. Check if the size estimation accounts for RLP encoding overhead correctly.
executable_checks: Assert final RLP block size <= MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE
Assert block builder refuses transactions that would exceed limit
Assert RLP encoding overhead is accounted for in size estimation
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-EIP7934-PRECOND-002. Pre-condition type. Only affects the individual validator; network rejects oversized blocks correctly. Related assumption ASSUM-EIP7934-03.

checklist_item.title: Verify serve range computation handles edge cases at FULU_FORK_EPOCH boundary
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. At epoch = FULU_FORK_EPOCH + 1: verify serve_range starts at FULU_FORK_EPOCH (not 0 or negative). 2. At epoch >> FULU_FORK_EPOCH: verify serve_range starts at current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS. 3. At epoch = FULU_FORK_EPOCH: verify serve_range is [FULU_FORK_EPOCH, FULU_FORK_EPOCH]. 4. Test underflow: if current_epoch < MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS, the max() clamp must prevent negative epochs.
executable_checks: Assert serve range floor = max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH)
Assert no integer underflow when current_epoch < MIN_EPOCHS
Assert serve range at FULU_FORK_EPOCH is valid
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-FULU-REQRESP-PRECOND-02. Supporting node check for serve range computation. Integer underflow risk when current_epoch < MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS.

checklist_item.title: Verify complete Fulu fork activation with all new state fields initialized
checklist_item.bug_class: Incomplete Fork Activation / State Initialization Failure
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Process blocks up to the Fulu fork epoch. 2. After the fork transition, verify: (a) state.fork.current_version == FULU_FORK_VERSION, (b) state.fork.previous_version == ELECTRA_FORK_VERSION, (c) state.fork.epoch == FULU_FORK_EPOCH, (d) state.proposer_lookahead is initialized and non-empty. 3. Verify that the first block after fork uses Fulu processing rules (not Electra). 4. Test: process a block that is only valid under Fulu rules (e.g., with new blob limits). Verify acceptance. 5. Process a block that would only be valid under pre-Fulu rules. Verify rejection.
executable_checks: Assert fork version fields correct after transition
Assert proposer_lookahead initialized and populated
Assert Fulu processing rules active for first post-fork block
Assert pre-Fulu-only valid blocks rejected post-fork
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-FULU-FORK-TRANSITION-POST-003. Post-condition type. Incomplete fork activation would cause all blocks after the fork to be processed incorrectly, affecting all nodes.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for BOUNDARY-TX-GAS-LIMIT-MEMPOOL: reject transactions exceeding gas cap
checklist_item.bug_class: Gas Cap Bypass / Transaction Pool Pollution
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Submit a transaction with gasLimit = 16,777,217 (2^24 + 1) via P2P. Verify rejection. 2. Submit with gasLimit = 16,777,216 (exactly 2^24). Verify acceptance. 3. Submit with gasLimit = MAX_UINT64. Verify rejection. 4. Monitor: does the rejected transaction get propagated to any peer? It must NOT. 5. Submit via JSON-RPC (if possible): verify it's also rejected at mempool level. 6. Include an over-cap transaction in a block received via P2P. Verify the entire block is rejected.
executable_checks: Assert gasLimit = 2^24 + 1 is rejected at mempool
Assert gasLimit = 2^24 is accepted
Assert rejected transaction is NOT propagated to peers
Assert block containing over-cap transaction is rejected
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7825-TX-POSTCOND-001. Boundary property. Trust model edges BOUNDARY-TX-GAS-LIMIT-MEMPOOL and BOUNDARY-BLOCK-GAS-LIMIT-P2P: gasLimit checked at both mempool and block validation levels.

checklist_item.title: Verify CL gossip size limit correctly accounts for EIP-7934 RLP block size interactions
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Verify the CL gossip limit (10 MiB) is consistent with the EL MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE limit. 2. Construct a block at the EL RLP size limit. Verify its SSZ-wrapped CL representation is within the 10 MiB gossip limit. 3. If the EL limit allows blocks that exceed the CL gossip limit after SSZ wrapping, this is a split risk. 4. Verify both limits account for all encoding overhead.
executable_checks: Assert EL max RLP block fits within CL 10 MiB gossip limit after wrapping
Assert no block can be EL-valid but CL-gossip-rejected due to size
Assert encoding overhead is accounted for in limit calculations
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-EIP7935-SECBND-POSTCOND-001. Supporting node check. Size limit coordination between EL (EIP-7934 RLP limit) and CL (10 MiB gossip limit) must be consistent.

checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary handling at ACTION-CHECK-FINALIZED-DESCENDANT

reachability.notes: Attacker crafts blocks that don't descend from finalized checkpoint to create alternative chains.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. After data crosses boundary at EDGE-FINALIZED-SLOT-OK-TO-ANCESTRY, verify internal handling at ACTION-CHECK-FINALIZED-DESCENDANT. 2. Check that validated data is correctly processed: A block must descend from the finalized checkpoint block: get_checkpoint_block must match store.finalized_checkpoint.root. 3. Verify no TOCTOU between boundary validation and internal use. 4. Confirm state transitions are correct post-validation.
executable_checks: Verify correct state transition at ACTION-CHECK-FINALIZED-DESCENDANT after boundary crossing
Check for TOCTOU vulnerabilities between validation and use
Confirm no data corruption in internal processing path
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-FULUFC-PRECOND-004. Supporting boundary check. Nodes under test: ['ACTION-CHECK-FINALIZED-DESCENDANT'].

checklist_item.title: Verify postcondition: After custody groups are assigned, the node must subscribe to exactly the gossip subnets corresponding to its custody gr

reachability.notes: Subscribing to wrong subnets would mean the node misses column data it should custody.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Execute the operation at ACTION-JOIN-SUBNETS. 2. Verify the postcondition: After custody groups are assigned, the node must subscribe to exactly the gossip subnets corresponding to its custody groups. 3. Check for unexpected side effects or state changes. 4. Test with edge-case inputs that stress the postcondition. 5. Cross-client comparison of output states.
executable_checks: Execute operation at ACTION-JOIN-SUBNETS with various inputs
Verify postcondition holds for all test cases
Check for unexpected side effects
Cross-client output comparison
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-DASCORE-POSTCOND-002. Mindset: Formal Verification Engineer. Type: Post-condition. Primary: ACTION-JOIN-SUBNETS. Nodes: ['ACTION-JOIN-SUBNETS', 'STATE-SUBSCRIBED-TO-SUBNETS']. Edges: ['EDGE-JOIN-SUBNETS', 'EDGE-SUBNETS-JOINED'].

checklist_item.title: Falsify invariant: Replace-by-fee (RBF) interactions with the sparse blobpool must not cause inconsistent propagation where some nodes have

reachability.notes: RBF impact on sparse blobpool is marked TBD in spec. Edge cases may exist where replaced transactions are inconsistently propagated.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the invariant at STATE-TX-IN-POOL: Replace-by-fee (RBF) interactions with the sparse blobpool must not cause inconsistent propagation where some nodes have the replacement and others have the original. 2. Design state transitions that could violate this invariant. 3. Test boundary conditions (min/max values, empty inputs, overflow). 4. Execute transitions and verify the invariant holds after each step. 5. If externally reachable, craft on-chain transactions or P2P messages to trigger violations. 6. Cross-client comparison to detect implementation divergence.
executable_checks: Construct state transition sequence targeting STATE-TX-IN-POOL
Test with boundary values (0, max, overflow)
Verify invariant holds across all transitions
Cross-client comparison if consensus-relevant
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-EIP8070-INV-006. Mindset: Formal Verification Engineer. Type: Invariant. Primary: STATE-TX-IN-POOL. Nodes: ['STATE-TX-IN-POOL']. Edges: ['EDGE-TO-POOL'].

checklist_item.title: Bypass precondition: Before publishing locally-produced data columns to gossip (ACTION-PUBLISH-IF-SUBSCRIBED), the node MUST verify it is sub

reachability.notes: Ensures nodes only publish on subscribed subnets

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the precondition at ACTION-PUBLISH-IF-SUBSCRIBED: Before publishing locally-produced data columns to gossip (ACTION-PUBLISH-IF-SUBSCRIBED), the node MUST verify it is subscribed to the target subnet (via custody or sampling). Only publish if subscribed, otherwise skip per EDGE-CONDITIONAL-PUBLISH condition. 2. Craft inputs that violate the precondition. 3. Verify the system correctly rejects invalid inputs. 4. Test edge cases: empty, null, max-value, type-confused inputs. 5. Check that rejection is consistent across implementations.
executable_checks: Send invalid inputs bypassing precondition at ACTION-PUBLISH-IF-SUBSCRIBED
Test edge cases (empty, null, overflow, type confusion)
Verify consistent rejection across clients
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-FULU-BLOB-PUB-PRECOND-03. Mindset: Formal Verification Engineer. Type: Pre-condition. Primary: ACTION-PUBLISH-IF-SUBSCRIBED. Nodes: ['ACTION-PUBLISH-IF-SUBSCRIBED']. Edges: ['EDGE-CONDITIONAL-PUBLISH'].

checklist_item.title: Falsify invariant: All nodes in the network must agree on FULU_FORK_EPOCH = 411392. Nodes activating at different epochs would create incom

reachability.notes: If a client ships with wrong FULU_FORK_EPOCH, all nodes running that client would fork at the wrong epoch.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the invariant at ACTION-CHECK-FORK-CONDITION: All nodes in the network must agree on FULU_FORK_EPOCH = 411392. Nodes activating at different epochs would create incompatible chains. 2. Design state transitions that could violate this invariant. 3. Test boundary conditions (min/max values, empty inputs, overflow). 4. Execute transitions and verify the invariant holds after each step. 5. If externally reachable, craft on-chain transactions or P2P messages to trigger violations. 6. Cross-client comparison to detect implementation divergence.
executable_checks: Construct state transition sequence targeting ACTION-CHECK-FORK-CONDITION
Test with boundary values (0, max, overflow)
Verify invariant holds across all transitions
Cross-client comparison if consensus-relevant
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-FULU-FORK-TRANSITION-INV-005. Mindset: Formal Verification Engineer. Type: Invariant. Primary: ACTION-CHECK-FORK-CONDITION. Nodes: ['ACTION-CHECK-FORK-CONDITION']. Edges: ['EDGE-CHECK-FORK']. [CONDITIONAL SCOPE: requires further investigation to confirm eligibility]

checklist_item.title: Bypass precondition: The upgrade_to_fulu function MUST only be invoked when the current slot is at an epoch boundary (slot % SLOTS_PER_EPOCH

reachability.notes: A bug in fork epoch detection could cause premature or delayed fork activation, leading to a network split if some clients fork at wrong epoch.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the precondition at ACTION-CHECK-FORK-EPOCH: The upgrade_to_fulu function MUST only be invoked when the current slot is at an epoch boundary (slot % SLOTS_PER_EPOCH == 0) AND the epoch equals FULU_FORK_EPOCH. 2. Craft inputs that violate the precondition. 3. Verify the system correctly rejects invalid inputs. 4. Test edge cases: empty, null, max-value, type-confused inputs. 5. Check that rejection is consistent across implementations.
executable_checks: Send invalid inputs bypassing precondition at ACTION-CHECK-FORK-EPOCH
Test edge cases (empty, null, overflow, type confusion)
Verify consistent rejection across clients
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6-FULU-FORK-ST-PRECOND-001. Mindset: Formal Verification Engineer. Type: Pre-condition. Primary: ACTION-CHECK-FORK-EPOCH. Nodes: ['ACTION-CHECK-FORK-EPOCH', 'ACTION-UPGRADE-TO-FULU']. Edges: ['EDGE-EPOCH-BOUNDARY-CHECK', 'EDGE-FORK-TRIGGERED'].

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-TX-SUBMIT: validate pre-condition at entry point

reachability.notes: Attacker can submit transactions via P2P with arbitrarily high gas limits.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the trust boundary at EDGE-TX-SUBMIT where untrusted data enters via P2P, Transaction. 2. Review input validation: A transaction submitted to the mempool MUST have its gas limit validated against the block gas limit (60M post-Fusaka) before acceptance. 3. Craft malicious input that violates the stated precondition. 4. Send via P2P, Transaction and observe whether the node accepts, rejects, or crashes. 5. Compare behavior across at least 2 client implementations for consensus divergence.
executable_checks: Craft malformed input targeting EDGE-TX-SUBMIT boundary
Send via P2P/transaction to a test node
Verify rejection with correct error code
Cross-client comparison of acceptance/rejection behavior
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-EIP7935-VAL-PRECOND-001. Mindset: Boundary Guard. checklist_item.title: Verify postcondition: After Electra fork activation, all 6 core CL EIPs (EIP-6110, EIP-7002, EIP-7251, EIP-7549, EIP-7685, EIP-7691) MUST be s

reachability.notes: Partial CL EIP activation would cause attestation/block validation disagreements.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Execute the operation at STATE-CL-POST-ELECTRA. 2. Verify the postcondition: After Electra fork activation, all 6 core CL EIPs (EIP-6110, EIP-7002, EIP-7251, EIP-7549, EIP-7685, EIP-7691) MUST be simultaneously active. 3. Check for unexpected side effects or state changes. 4. Test with edge-case inputs that stress the postcondition. 5. Cross-client comparison of output states.
executable_checks: Execute operation at STATE-CL-POST-ELECTRA with various inputs
Verify postcondition holds for all test cases
Check for unexpected side effects
Cross-client output comparison
